layman_headline: Ethereum looks oversold during a broader market fear selloff — time to nibble cautiously.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is pulling back as a global tech stock selloff and geopolitical tensions (Iran strikes) are dragging risk assets down. Bitcoin dropped below $63K and most altcoins are bleeding. However, Ethereum's RSI is approaching oversold territory at 35, and it actually managed a +2% gain over the past week despite today's dip. With Fear & Greed at 27, we're in the zone where historically buying fear has paid off, though the environment remains risky.
layman_action: Consider a small, cautious position in Ethereum around $1,790 with a strict stop-loss at $1,680. This is a contrarian play — buying during fear — so size it modestly and be prepared for more downside before any bounce.
expert_reasoning: ETH at RSI 35 is approaching the oversold zone that has historically produced strong bounces — and RSI is our most reliable signal source (80% win rate). The chart shows a bullish marubozu candle pattern with 5-day momentum of +6.5%, suggesting buyers are stepping in despite the broader bearish structure. Price sits just above $1,700 support with resistance at $1,800 and then open air to $2,000+. The macro selloff (AI stock rout, geopolitical tension) creates the fear that produces good swing entries. Ethereum's $40B TVL is stable, providing fundamental support. The risk/reward from $1,790 entry to $2,020 target vs $1,680 stop is approximately 2.1:1, meeting our minimum threshold.
risk_factors: Broader risk-off wave could intensify — Iran escalation or deeper tech selloff could push BTC below $60K and drag ETH to $1,600
BTC dominance at 56.3% and rising means capital continues flowing away from alts including ETH
ETH is below all major moving averages in a structural downtrend — this bounce could be a dead cat
watch_list: BTC price action around $62,000-63,000 support — a break below $60K would invalidate the ETH trade
Fear & Greed index trajectory — currently improving from 25 to 27, continued improvement supports the bounce thesis
Geopolitical developments (Iran situation) and US tech stock futures Monday for risk sentiment direction
market_context: Crypto markets are in a fear-driven selloff triggered by a global tech stock rout and geopolitical tensions. BTC is down 2% below $63K, most alts are down 3-10%, but improving Fear & Greed (25→27) suggests selling pressure may be peaking.
context_banner: Risk-off selloff creates a contrarian ETH entry near oversold RSI levels, but position sizing should reflect the uncertain macro backdrop.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 35 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal source at 80% historical win rate, suggesting a bounce setup is forming.
Reports of Bitcoin sagging under $62.5K due to Iran strikes — geopolitical fear-driven selloffs have historically been good contrarian entry points for quality assets.
Fear & Greed at 27 (Fear) but improving from 25 — the market is fearful enough for contrarian entries but the trend is stabilizing rather than worsening.
ETH derivatives reset article suggests leveraged positions have been flushed, reducing the risk of further cascading liquidations on the downside.
Risk-off wave dragging BTC below $63K as AI selloff spreads — this macro headwind is the key risk factor but may be near exhaustion given the improving fear gauge.
